preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the neutral schwa [ə] is a common historical and synchronic process involving syllabic nasals. When a nasal consonant becomes the nucleus of a syllable (due to the loss of surrounding vowels), it often develops a supporting epenthetic schwa to assist in articulation, or vocalizes entirely into a vowel in certain language branches.
phoneticEffects: Vocalization, Syllabic Nasal Development, Vowel Epenthesis.

examples.note: Synchronic alternation between a syllabic nasal and a schwa-nasal sequence.
Historical reduction of the final syllable to a schwa + nasal.
Historical vocalization where the PIE syllabic nasal shifted to a low/central vowel (compare Latin in-).
